items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.

items.q: According to the inverted-U hypothesis, when is performance best?
items.correct: At an optimal moderate level of arousal.
items.why: Students assume 'more psyched up = better'. The inverted-U shows performance rises with arousal up to an optimum, then DECLINES with excess arousal (the athlete chokes). The optimum varies by skill.

items.q: Which is the strongest source of self-efficacy?
items.why: It's Mastery experience. “Vicarious experience” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: Technique and effort under athlete's control
items.why: It's Technique and effort under athlete's control. “Final ranking” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If a SMART goal is to improve a 5 km time from 25:00 to 22:30 over 12 weeks, weekly improvement (s) is?
items.why: The real figure is 12.5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
